title: Red Hat Bug Fix Advisory: selinux-policy bug fix and enhancement update
overview: An update for selinux-policy is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.
description: For detailed information on changes in this release, see the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 Release Notes linked from the References section.
affected_products: Red Hat Enterprise Linux for x86_64 10 x86_64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for Power, little endian
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for ARM 64
Red Hat Enterprise Linux for IBM z Systems

fixes.description: afterburn.service fails in RHEL-9.6 (RHCOS) with SELinux enabled
[rhel-9] SELinux prevents chronyc from talking to chronyd-restricted via socket
AVC generated by "systemd-user-runtime-dir stop 0" service when having directories with various contexts
[rhel-9] the varnish service triggers SELinux denials
[rhel-9] SELinux denials appear when NetworkManager executes ping
[rhel-9] 'journalctl -M tux' fails due to AVC denied
[rhel-9] tlog lock files can't be created by confined SELinux users
SELinux policy, preventing PAM stack execution when login executes.
Selinux: NetworkManager is denied to create temporary keyfile
SELinux prevents haproxy from mmap-ing /dev/shm/haproxy_startup_logs_* files
[rhel-9] SELinux denies NetworkManager to kill nft/iptables process
[c9s] bootupctl adopt get selinux denied logs
[RHEL-9.7] New SELinux domain required for TDX confidential virtualization "qgs" daemon
selinux reports avc denied during installation of coreos-installer-bootinfra
SELinux prevents systemd_timedated_t to start/stop timemaster_unit_file_t
In RHEL9 SELinux prevents Postfix tlsproxy from accessing TCP sockets (TLS handshake failure)
Revert selinux-policy rules to back insights-core policy module [rhel-9]

full_text:
An update for selinux-policy is now available for Red Hat Enterprise Linux 10.

For detailed information on changes in this release, see the Red Hat Enterprise Linux 9 Release Notes linked from the References section.
